title: nephrodium praetervisum
intro: Nephrodium praetervisum, also known as the Black Fern, is a species of extinct plant that belonged to the family Matoniaceae. The Black Fern was likely used in traditional medicine due to its medicinal properties, which include its antiseptic and anti-inflammatory properties. Its health benefits are said to include the treatment of skin conditions, wounds, and respiratory issues, making it a valuable resource for ancient cultures. The herbal preparations of Nephrodium praetervisum were typically made by infusing the plant's leaves in water or oil to create topical treatments for various health concerns.
